msg: Task 3.1 plan spec used plain path.resolve() to compare CLI-arg worktree paths against git worktree list --porcelain output, which prints realpaths; on macOS mktemp -d returns /var/... while git reports /private/var/..., causing spurious NOT_FOUND. Added a resolveRealOrAbs() helper (realpathSync with resolve fallback) and used it for the repoRoot-exclusion filter, --worktree-path matching, and the duplicate-path guard.

Plan Task 3.1 fixture spec implied exit 2 (invalid slug) for a 10000-char oversized slug hostile case, but SLUG_RE has no length bound so an all-lowercase 10000-char slug is valid syntax and correctly falls through to NOT_FOUND (exit 1). Adjusted fixture assertion to accept exit in {1,2} and confirmed no hang, matching the actual correct behavior rather than a hypothetical exit code.

cmdWorktreeReconcile uses resolveRealOrAbs() (Wave-3 helper) instead of plain path.resolve() for all worktree-path comparisons, since macOS mktemp -d paths under /tmp are realpath /private/tmp in git worktree list --porcelain output, which would otherwise misclassify in-sync worktrees as stale or as spurious adopt candidates in fixtures.

a1-reconcile fixture suite rewrites its own committed drift-*.md fixture timestamps in place on every run (test-side-effect pollution), unrelated to Wave 4 which only touched a1-worktree; consider mktemp-isolating reconcile fixtures per CONVENTIONS.md Isolation section

Task 5.1 plan assumed _test-fixtures/a1-pr-review/run-test.sh already followed the pass/fail-counter + last-line-summary convention (CONVENTIONS.md), but it used ad-hoc pass()/fail() echo helpers with set -euo pipefail and a plain All tests passed. line, which does not satisfy the plans own done-when (tail -1 | grep -q 0 failed). Converted the whole file to the standard pass=0 fail=0 counter/summary pattern, keeping all 8 pre-existing test bodies behavior-identical, before appending the new direct-path and hostile-input cases.
